hypothesis: Under atmospheric CO2 partial pressure (~0.4 mbar) and moderate humidity (1–3% water vapor at 298 K), linear polyamine polymers whose repeat units comprise >60% secondary amines will exhibit ≥15% higher equilibrium CO2 uptake (mmol g−1) in long-timescale MD+GCMC simulations than structurally similar polymers dominated (>60%) by primary or tertiary amines, whereas under dry conditions (<0.1% water vapor) this advantage will shrink to <5%.
